items.title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning

items.title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers
items.summary: Controllable image generation remains challenging for creative professionals, who often require precise regional control over materials, object identities, and spatial arrangements that cannot be reliably achieved through text prompting alone. Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) can na…

items.title: CodeRescue: Budget-Calibrated Recovery Routing for Coding Agents
items.summary: Coding agents increasingly operate in executable environments where a failed attempt produces actionable feedback rather than merely an incorrect answer. Existing cost-aware systems typically treat such failures as cascade decisions: try a cheap model first, then escalate hard ca…

items.title: 1-Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds
items.summary: Controlling the Lipschitz constant of a neural network is a standard way to promote robustness and stability. Most existing constraining strategies are designed for Euclidean spaces. In this work, we construct and analyze a class of 1-Lipschitz neural networks on Hadamard manifol…

items.title: Fundamental limits of distributed multiclass classification from simple binary decisions
items.summary: We consider the problem of constructing a $K$-class classifier from the combination of $O(\log K)$ simple binary classifiers -- this is a natural paradigm to construct a sophisticated classifier in a distributed manner with each agent performing a relatively straightforward task.…

items.title: Provable diffusion-based posterior sampling for linear inverse problems via DDIM
items.summary: Diffusion-based methods have achieved remarkable empirical success in solving inverse problems. However, many existing posterior samplers either lack rigorous theoretical guarantees or incur substantial computational overhead. We propose a simple and efficient algorithm, called \…

items.title: Associative Emotional Learning in Convolutional Neural Networks

items.title: Selective State-Space Adaptation and Retrieval for Language Model Reasoning
items.summary: Low-rank adaptation introduces a static learned update applied identically to every input. The update provides task-level adaptation but does not explicitly represent token-level or instance-level state variation. A family of adapters is proposed that introduces selective state-s…

items.title: Two-Level Meta-Rubrics for Evaluating Open-Ended Generation: GAMUT, a Benchmark for Factual Completeness
